{{Quote|Yousa have returned oursa Staff! Wesa will do as yousa ask, Gallo.|Tenko, to Gallo, upon the retrieval of their village's Sacred Staff|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
'''Tenko''' was a [[Ankura/Legends|Ankura]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] and the [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] of the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Otoh Jahai]]. During a [[War of the Gungan tribes|war]] between the Gungan tribes in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3000 BBY]], Boss [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]] of [[Otoh Sancture]] approached Tenko with a request that they stop fighting other tribes and join their forces to Gallo's cause: defeating the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] Boss [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]]. Tenko agreed to help only if Gallo returned their city's [[Sacred Staff]] from the city of [[Otoh Langua/Legends|Otoh Langua]]. After Gallo discovered that the [[Staff/Legends|staff]] was not in the possession of Otoh Langua, but rather located in the [[Tree/Legends|forest]], and returned the staff to Tenko, the Otoh Jahai Boss did as promised and sent [[Troop/Legends|troops]] to join Gallo's [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Grand Army]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Otoh Langua stolen oursa sacred staff. If theysa return it, then wesa might talk peace.|Tenko, to Gallo|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
[[File:Otoh_Jatai.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Boss Tenko in Otoh Jahai with the Sacred Staff]]
Tenko was a [[Ankura/Legends|Ankura]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] with [[Color/Legends|green]] skin from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] and the [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] of the Gungan [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Otoh Jahai]] by<ref name="SW:GB" /> the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3000 BBY]],<ref name="EA" /> a [[Time/Legends|time]] when many Gungan cities were fighting each other. During an effort to unite the Gungan villages and defeat the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] Boss [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]] in the [[War of the Gungan tribes|war]] between the Gungan tribes, Boss [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]] of [[Otoh Sancture]] approached Tenko, asking for a stop to the inter-tribe violence and assistance in his quest to avenge his city, which had been [[Destruction of Otoh Sancture|destroyed]] by [[Bursa/Legends|bursas]] under Rogoe's control. Tenko agreed but only if Gallo promised to return the [[Sacred Staff]] stolen from their village by the people of [[Otoh Langua/Legends|Otoh Langua]], a charge denied by Boss [[Hantic]], their leader. When Gallo discovered the missing [[Staff/Legends|staff]] in the [[Tree/Legends|forest]], not in the possession of Otoh Langua, he returned it to Otoh Jahai, and Tenko agreed to halt attacks on Otoh Langua and lend the support of their [[Troop/Legends|troops]] to Gallo's cause by donating four heavy [[Militiagung/Legends|militiagungs]]. With the support of Tenko and the newly formed [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]], Gallo went on to unite all the Gungan tribes and [[Raid on Spearhead|defeat]] Rogoe, thus ending the war.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Personality and traits==
Tenko was unwilling to stand down from a fight if their people were at risk. They were also quick to assume the people of the village of Otoh Langua were responsible for the loss of their village's Sacred Staff. Once proven wrong, though, they made sure to stick to their promise and was willing to lend troops to Boss Gallo's cause.<ref name="SW:GB" /> The Gungan's surname was a term used to refer to a sharp [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]].<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Boss Tenko made their first and only appearances in the [[2001]] [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' and its expansion pack, [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']]. Tenko appears in the second mission of the Gungan campaign, titled "The Gungan Who Would Be Boss," and their character icon is the same as most other Gungan Boss character icons in the game. No voice actor is credited for Tenko in the game's credits.<ref name="SW:GB" /> The [[Prima Games]] [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] for the game indirectly mentions Tenko as a voice the player hears as they enter the city of Otoh Jahai.<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>
